response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded software background, but the role demands deep hardware-level expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no exposure to deep neural network optimization, SIMD/MIMD architectures, performance modeling of silicon, or chip design collaboration. The educational credential (BS) meets the 8+ years bar nominally, but the experience is in the wrong domain entirely.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering experience across diverse stacks
Some low-level C/C++ work at National Instruments and high-performance DLang systems at Sociomantic
Demonstrated engineering rigor (TDD, CI/CD, performance-sensitive backend services)
concerns: No SoC modeling, IP modeling, or chip architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product engineering, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload optimization background
Long engineering tenure with some low-level C/C++ and high-performance systems work
